title: DisplayManagerService overrideHdrTypes() zero permission check
notes: CROSS-VERIFIED CONFIRMED. AIDL comment says 'Requires ACCESS_SURFACE_FLINGER' but no @EnforcePermission annotation, no enforcePermission() call. Adjacent method requestColorMode has proper @EnforcePermission('CONFIGURE_DISPLAY_COLOR_MODE'). overrideHdrTypes(int displayId, int[] modes) has NO permission check. Compare: setUserDisabledHdrTypes and setAreUserDisabledHdrTypesAllowed both require WRITE_SECURE_SETTINGS. Any app can call overrideHdrTypes to modify SurfaceFlinger display HDR config. Can disable HDR or set bogus types causing rendering issues. Directly calls DisplayControl.overrideHdrTypes(displayToken, modes).
